skill.name: lark-workflow-meeting-summary
skill.description: Summarizes Lark meeting records for a selected date range and prepares structured meeting reports.
skill.summary: Create structured Lark meeting summaries, weekly reports, and optional Lark documents from meeting notes.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Ruby backtick and path traversal patterns, but the reviewed file is Markdown workflow guidance, not executable Ruby source. The external command findings are mostly legitimate lark-cli examples, while the relative path, weak cryptography, and entropy findings appear to be false positives. Medium risk remains because the workflow can access Lark meeting metadata, note document links, transcripts, and can create or update Lark documents with authenticated user credentials.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Lark CLI Commands Access Meeting Data
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The workflow instructs the agent to authenticate with lark-cli, search meetings, retrieve note metadata, and batch query document links. This is expected for the skill, but it can expose sensitive meeting records, note document tokens, and transcript document tokens to the model output or downstream documents.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly shown and operate on authenticated Lark meeting and document data. The pattern is legitimate for the workflow, but the data sensitivity makes the risk real.

medium_findings.title: Optional Document Creation Or Update With Meeting Content
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The workflow includes commands to create a Lark document or append content to an existing document. This is a legitimate feature, but mistakes in target document selection or user consent could publish confidential meeting summaries to the wrong location.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The document write commands are directly documented and use user credentials. The risk depends on user confirmation and destination controls, so the finding is medium rather than high.

low_findings.title: Markdown Relative Links Trigger Path Traversal Alerts
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The ../ references are Markdown links to sibling skill documentation, not code that reads arbitrary user-controlled paths. No evidence found of unsanitized filesystem access or path traversal execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is visible Markdown documentation with fixed relative links. There is no file-reading code path or user input involved in those locations.

low_findings.title: Backtick Pattern Alerts Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The Ruby backtick alerts are caused by Markdown code fences and inline code examples. The file does instruct use of shell and lark-cli commands, but there is no Ruby code or dynamic command execution syntax in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review shows Markdown fences and inline command examples rather than executable Ruby backtick expressions. The external command workflow is separately captured as a medium operational risk.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography And Entropy Alerts Lack Supporting Evidence
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static weak cryptography alert points to the YAML description line, and the entropy alert points to the start of a short Chinese Markdown file. No cryptographic operation, binary payload, encrypted blob, or obfuscated content was found in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain front matter and a Chinese description. There is no visible code or encoded payload to support the weak cryptography or obfuscation interpretations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell And Cloud CLI Operations
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on system date commands and lark-cli operations for authentication, meeting search, note lookup, metadata queries, and document writes. These commands should run only after user authorization and with care around sensitive meeting data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and central to the skill. They are not malicious by themselves, but they access external services using the user account.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time collecting meeting notes across many Lark calls. This skill searches a date range, gathers note links, and prepares a structured meeting report.
actual_capabilities: Guides authentication for Lark VC and Drive access through lark-cli.
Searches Lark meetings within a user-specified date range.
Collects meeting IDs, note document tokens, and transcript document tokens.
Queries Lark Drive metadata to get note and transcript document links.
Formats daily or weekly meeting reports with meeting details and availability status.
Can create or append a Lark document when the user asks for document output.
limitations: Requires lark-cli and a valid authenticated Lark user account.
Meeting search ranges longer than one month must be split into separate queries.
Some meetings may not have available notes or transcript documents.
It does not summarize full document bodies unless the needed Drive access is available.

use_cases.title: Weekly Team Meeting Report
use_cases.description: Collect the last week of Lark meetings and produce a report with topics, links, and missing notes.

use_cases.title: Daily Meeting Review
use_cases.description: Review today or yesterday meetings and identify which notes and transcripts are available.

use_cases.title: Meeting Archive Preparation
use_cases.description: Create a Lark document that lists meeting summaries and note links for a project period.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize This Week
prompt_templates.prompt: Summarize my Lark meetings for this week. Include meeting times, titles, note links, transcript links, and missing notes.

prompt_templates.title: Create Daily Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Lark meetings from today. Group them by time and mark which meetings have notes or transcripts.

prompt_templates.title: Build Project Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a meeting summary report for the project meetings from May 1 to May 15. Highlight decisions, blockers, and missing note documents.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Lark Document
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a Lark document with the last seven days of meeting summaries. Confirm the target title before creating or updating the document.

output_examples.output: 今日会议概览: includes each meeting time, subject, note link, transcript link, and a label for meetings without notes.

output_examples.input: Generate a weekly meeting report for the last seven days.
output: Meeting count and note availability summary.
Daily sections with meeting titles and links.
A final list of meetings that need follow-up notes.

output_examples.input: Create a Lark document for this month meetings.
output_examples.output: A structured document draft with the requested date range, meeting details, and source links ready for confirmation.

best_practices: Confirm the date range before running Lark meeting searches.
Review note and transcript links before sharing the final report.
Ask for confirmation before creating or updating any Lark document.
anti_patterns: Do not run the workflow without a signed-in Lark user account.
Do not publish meeting summaries to a document without checking the destination.
Do not assume missing notes exist when the API reports no notes available.

faq.question: What does this skill summarize?
faq.answer: It summarizes Lark meeting records, note links, transcript links, and availability status for a selected date range.

faq.question: Does it require Lark authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow requires lark-cli authentication with VC access, and Drive access for document links or document creation.

faq.question: Can it create a Lark document?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create or append a document when the user asks and confirms the destination.

faq.question: Can it handle long date ranges?
faq.answer: Yes, but meeting searches longer than one month must be split into multiple queries.

faq.question: Will every meeting have notes?
faq.answer: No. Some meetings may return no notes available, and the report should mark them clearly.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it uses authenticated commands to access sensitive meeting data.
